functions.name: JSON.GetArrayItemsAsQuickList
functions.description: Queries array values as QuickList. Creates a new QuickList and puts each JSON there into an entry of the QuickList. See QuickList.JoinJSON function to join back the JSON.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

functions.result: Returns quicklist refernece number or error.

examples.label: Try the function:
examples.text_code:
Let([
list =
MBS
( "JSON.GetArrayItemsAsQuickList"; "[{\"id\": 1},{\"id\": 2},{\"id\": 3}]" );
text =
MBS
( "
QuickList.GetList
"; list );
r =
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
"; list )
]; text)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
list = MBS( "JSON.GetArrayItemsAsQuickList"; "[{\"id\": 1},{\"id\": 2},{\"id\": 3}]" );
text = MBS( "QuickList.GetList"; list );
r = MBS( "QuickList.Release"; list )
]; text)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Filter JSON items:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$json
; Value: "[{\"id\": 1},{\"id\": 2},{\"id\": 3}]" ]
# turn into a list
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "JSON.GetArrayItemsAsQuickList";
$json
) ]
#
# now loop over entries to filter
Set Variable [
$n
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Count
";
$list
) ]
If [
$n
> 0 ]
Loop
# get next item
Set Variable [
$n
; Value:
$n
- 1 ]
Set Variable [
$item
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.GetValue
";
$list
;
$n
) ]
#
# if meets criteria, then remove it
If [ JSONGetElement (
$item
; "id" ) = 2 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Remove
";
$list
;
$n
) ]
End If
#
# next
Exit Loop If [
$n
≤ 0 ]
End Loop
End If
#
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.JoinJSON
";
$list
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "after filter" ;
$json
]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
";
$list
) ]

  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>$n - 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$n</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QuickList.GetValue"; $list; $n ) </Calculation>

functions.name: JSON.GetArrayPathItems
functions.description: Queries value from JSON path in array of objects. The path describes the way through the hierarchy to find the JSON node to return. Result can be JSON or the value.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference. If that is not an array, the function returns an empty result.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path through the json hierarchy as list. For object, pass name of item to choose. For array pass index.

parameters.name: AsValue
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return as value, e.g. text or number. Pass 0 (or nothing) to return as JSON.

examples.label: Query all test values in objects in array:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

examples.label: Filter items in JSON:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
// input may come from caller
input = "[{\"land\": \"CH\"}, {\"land\": \"CH\"}, {\"land\": \"DE\"}, {\"land\": \"AT\"}, {\"land\": \"CH\"}, {\"land\": \"CH\"}]"; 

// parse the JSON
j = MBS("JSON.Parse"; input);
// loop over items backwards
c = MBS("JSON.GetArraySize"; j);
r = While ( [ i = c-1 ] ; i >= 0 ; [ 
	// query a value and if it doesn't match
	r = If( MBS( "JSON.GetPathItem"; j; i & ¶ & "land"; 1 ) <> "CH" ; 	
		// remove an item
		MBS( "JSON.DeleteItemFromArray"; j; i ); ""); i = i - 1 ] ; "" );
// Format and return JSON
r = MBS("JSON.Format"; j);
x = MBS("JSON.Release"; j)
]; r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Extract error details from JSON:
examples.text_code:
# JSON in field:
#{
# "error": {
#   "message": "Your card's expiration year is invalid.",
#   "type": "card_error",
#   "param": "exp_year",
#   "code": "invalid_expiry_year"
# }
#}
#get it in variable
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
test::input
]
#pick error node
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.GetObjectItem";
$json
; "error")]
If [
$error
= ""]
Show Custom Dialog ["No error"; "No error node in the JSON."]
Else
#get message node
Set Variable [
$message
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.GetObjectItem";
$error
; "message")]
#decode text
Set Variable [
$messageText
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.GetStringValue
";
$message
)]
#get code node
Set Variable [
$code
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.GetObjectItem";
$error
; "code")]
#decode text
Set Variable [
$codeText
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.GetStringValue
";
$code
)]
#show result
Show Custom Dialog ["Got error"; "Message: " &
$messageText
& ¶ & "Code: " &
$codeText
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> JSON in field:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>{</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> "error": {</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>   "message": "Your card's expiration year is invalid.",</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>   "type": "card_error",</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>   "param": "exp_year",</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>   "code": "invalid_expiry_year"</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> }</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>}</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>get it in variable</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>test::input</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>pick error node</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetObjectItem"; $json; "error")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$error = ""</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"No error"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"No error node in the JSON."</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>get message node</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetObjectItem"; $error; "message")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$message</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>decode text</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetStringValue"; $message)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$messageText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>get code node</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetObjectItem"; $error; "code")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$code</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>decode text</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetStringValue"; $code)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$codeText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>show result</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Got error"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Message: " & $messageText & ¶ & "Code: " & $codeText</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.GetObjectItemTree
functions.description: Queries named item from an object tree. You still need functions like JSON.GetStringValue to get back the decoded string. Works also for arrays where you pass in index. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Names parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Names...
parameters.description: The names for keys to query. Pass several parameters to go down an object tree. For arrays you can pass index.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.GetObjectItemTreeRef
functions.description: Queries named item from an object tree. You still need functions like JSON.GetStringValue to get back the decoded string. Works also for arrays where you pass in index. Same as JSON.GetObjectItemTree , but returns reference number. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Names parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Names...
parameters.description: The names for keys to query. Pass several parameters to go down an object tree. For arrays you can pass index.

functions.name: JSON.GetPathItem
functions.description: Queries value from JSON path. The path describes the way through the hierarchy to find the JSON node to return. Result can be JSON or the value. If path ends with # in an array, we return number of elements. Implemented FileMaker syntax in v12.4 for JSON element get/set like in JSONGetElement function, so you can use dot notation, [array index] or quoted text in brackets.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path through the json hierarchy as list. For object, pass name of item to choose. For array pass index.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return as value, e.g. text or number. Pass 0 (or nothing) to return as JSON. Add 2 to return empty in case of error, e.g. value not existing. (new in 7.5)

examples.label: Query IP via ipinfo.io:
examples.text_code:
Go to Layout [“Table”]
New Record/Request
#Start new session
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
")]
#Set URL to load (HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, FTPS, SFTP, etc.)
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
; "http://ipinfo.io")]
#RUN now
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
)]
#Check result
Set Field [
Table::DebugLog
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
)]
Set Field [
Table::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetResultAsText
";
$curl
; "UTF8")]
If [
$result
= "OK"]
#Check HTTP error code
Set Variable [
$response
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.GetResponseCode
";
$curl
)]
If [
$response
= 200]
#Query values from JSON
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.Parse
";
Table::Result
)]
Set Field [
Table::IP
;
MBS
("JSON.GetPathItem";
$json
; "ip"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::Hostname
;
MBS
("JSON.GetPathItem";
$json
; "hostname"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::City
;
MBS
("JSON.GetPathItem";
$json
; "city"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::Region
;
MBS
("JSON.GetPathItem";
$json
; "region"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::Country
;
MBS
("JSON.GetPathItem";
$json
; "country"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::Location
;
MBS
("JSON.GetPathItem";
$json
; "loc"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::Company
or Provider;
MBS
("JSON.GetPathItem";
$json
; "org"; 1)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.Release
";
$json
)]
End If
End If
#Cleanup
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
)]
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="Table"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Start new session</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$curl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Set URL to load (HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, FTPS, SFTP, etc.)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionURL"; $curl; "http://ipinfo.io")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>RUN now</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Perform"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Check result</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="DebugLog"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetResultAsText"; $curl; "UTF8")</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$result = "OK"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Check HTTP error code</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetResponseCode"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$response</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$response = 200</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Query values from JSON</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Parse"; Table::Result )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "ip"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="IP"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "hostname"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Hostname"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem";  $json; "city"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="City"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "region"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Region"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "country"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Country"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "loc"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Location"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "org"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Company or Provider"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Release"; $json )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Release"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Extract error details from JSON:
examples.text_code:
# JSON in field:
#{
# "error": {
#   "message": "Your card's expiration year is invalid.",
#   "type": "card_error",
#   "param": "exp_year",
#   "code": "invalid_expiry_year"
# }
#}
#get it in variable
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
test::input
]
#pick error node
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.GetObjectItem
";
$json
; "error")]
If [
$error
= ""]
Show Custom Dialog ["No error"; "No error node in the JSON."]
Else
#get message node
Set Variable [
$message
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.GetObjectItem
";
$error
; "message")]
#decode text
Set Variable [
$messageText
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.GetStringValue";
$message
)]
#get code node
Set Variable [
$code
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.GetObjectItem
";
$error
; "code")]
#decode text
Set Variable [
$codeText
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.GetStringValue";
$code
)]
#show result
Show Custom Dialog ["Got error"; "Message: " &
$messageText
& ¶ & "Code: " &
$codeText
]
End If
examples.xml_code:

    <Text>   "code": "invalid_expiry_year"</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> }</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>}</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>get it in variable</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>test::input</Calculation>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">

  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">

      </Button>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>get message node</Text>
  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetObjectItem"; $error; "message")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$message</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>decode text</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetStringValue"; $message)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$messageText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>get code node</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

  </Step>

functions.name: JSON.Import
functions.signature: MBS( "JSON.Import"; JSON { ; InsertFileName; TableNamePrefix; RootTableName; Flags } )
functions.description: Imports JSON and creates tables, fields and records. This function can import any JSON into FileMaker tables with all fields and attributes. Returns number of records to be added. This function prefers the import and runs it later at idle time. In a script please call JSON.Import and then loop and do script pauses. Then use JSON.Import.Status to check regularly for updates. When you get back Finished as status, the script can continue with other work. If you have a JSON file you need to import regularly into FileMaker and you have no XSLT to transform it for FileMaker, you can use this plugin function. On the first run, it creates for a sample JSON file the required tables and fields. Then you can define any layout or script to process values, e.g. copy into your tables. On further runs the plugin may add more fields (if newer JSON file has more fields) and import newer records. All tables have three default fields. _RecordUUID is an unique identifier for the record. _ParentRecordUUID provides a link to the record one level higher in the JSON. This can be used to find child/parent records. The _CreationTimeStamp is the creation date, so you can distinguish different imports. As a lot of records with a lot of data is imported and you may not need everything, you may want to put the imports in an extra FileMaker file. You decide if you clear tables before import or you want to keep history of all imports. If the JSON has errors like a missing < in the JSON, you get an error. If you switch on IgnoreError parameter, the plugin reads everything until the error is reached which may give less records than possible. This function works at idle time which is not supported on server. But you can use the function JSON.Import.Work to perform the work instead of the idle handler. This enables importing of data, but not table/field creation. Requires FileMaker 12 or newer. On Server, please add a loop calling JSON.Import.Work to make progress. See also XML.Import.SetBaseFields and XML.Import.SetExtraField . We use SQL to create records, so you can check FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL after the import for SQL errors. Please wait for an import to finish before calling JSON.Import or XML.Import again since it will cancel the last import and start a new one. Import fails if a table or field name matches a reserved word in FileMaker like RowId. Please rename entries if needed. Since v13.5 you can explicit ask the plugin to not create UUID fields with the flag value 1024. Be aware, that this prevents us from giving you structural data like which child record belongs to which parent. But your import data may already have primary keys for this. Add flag 512 to ignore duplicate errors in FileMaker's field validation. This is useful if you mark some field as unique in FileMaker with always validation and import the same data again. You can use JSON.Search or JSON.Query to preprocess the JSON and extract only the parts you like to import. Since v15.1 you can use flag 2048 to ask the plugin to check field types for the insert operation. If the field is number, we convert text to number and we may replace dot with comma if needed. For time, date and timestamp we ask FileMaker to convert which may depend on the current locale in the script. If the JSON contains numeric values, we create decimal fields. You may need to yourself to change field type from text to time, date or timestamp if you prefer that as field types.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The JSON data to import. Please pass text, not reference numbers.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty.

parameters.name: TableNamePrefix
parameters.description: The prefix to put in front of all tables.

parameters.name: RootTableName
parameters.description: The default root table name. Default is "json". Can be "-" to only use the table name prefix (since version 16.1).

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Sum of flags. Add 128 to skip table creation. Add 256 to skip field creation. Add 512 to ignore duplicate errors for FileMaker's validation (since v13.5). Add 1024 to not create UUID fields (since v13.5). Add 2048 to handle field types. (since 15.1)

examples.label: Imports test.json into FileMaker:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
("
Text.ReadTextFile
"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.json"; "UTF-8")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.Import";
$text
; ""; "")]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:""]

functions.name: JSON.Import.Cancel
functions.description: Cancels current import.

functions.name: JSON.Import.ErrorCount
functions.description: Queries error counter. Should be zero if we had no errors while importing.

functions.name: JSON.Import.Errors
functions.description: Queries list of error messages. We remove duplicates, so you may not see all errors.

functions.name: JSON.Import.SetBaseFields
functions.description: Sets the names of the base fields per record. If the names are empty, the field is not created.

parameters.name: RecordUUIDFieldName
parameters.description: The field name for the record UUID field.

parameters.name: CreationTimeStampFieldName
parameters.description: The name for the time stamp field.

parameters.name: ParentRecordUUIDFieldName
parameters.description: The name for the parent record UUID field.

examples.label: Disable all fields:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.Import.SetBaseFields"; ""; ""; "")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.Import.SetBaseFields"; "_RecordUUID"; "_CreationTimeStamp"; "_ParentRecordUUID")</Calculation>
    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.Import.SetExtraField
functions.description: Sets the extra field to add to all tables. Please call before JSON.Import . You can call it again when import loop is done and pass in empty name and value to clear it. Only one extra field and only added when table is created.

parameters.name: FieldName
parameters.description: The name of the extra field.

parameters.name: FieldValue
parameters.description: The value for this field. Can be any data type, but must match field type.

parameters.name: FieldType
parameters.description: The SQL field type. Default is "VARCHAR" for a text field.

examples.label: Set extra field:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Import.SetExtraField"; "test"; "Hello"; "VARCHAR") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.Import.Status
functions.description: Queries status of xml import. Returns Working, Finished or Unknown depending of the state. So before first import, it is unknown. Then with first import it switches to working. Now when it's ready, you get back Finished.

functions.name: JSON.Import.Todo
functions.description: Queries how many items are to be done for XML import.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to return. By default we return number of records to create. You can pass here "Tables" to get number of tables, "Fields" to get number of fields or "Records" to get number of records.

functions.name: JSON.Import.Total
functions.description: Queries total items to be imported.

functions.name: JSON.Import.Work
functions.description: Performs import. Normally we do import on idle while a script pause. But on server this does not work. You can call this method in a loop to do the inserts for the import. But it will not create the tables and fields required for import. Changed in version 11.3 to use per thread data, so multiple scripts on server can do imports independently. Please make sure you call all JSON.Import functions on the same script, so they can access their own local dataset and not interfere with other scripts doing imports. We use SQL to create records, so you can check FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL after the import for SQL errors.

examples.label: Sample loop:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$counter
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
If [
MBS
("IsServer") ]
# do some work now
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.Import.Work") ]
# let server do indexing
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
Else
# in FileMaker Pro do it in the pause
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
End If
# and check if we are done
Set Variable [
$todo
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.Import.Todo
") ]
Set Variable [
$s
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.Import.Status
") ]
Exit Loop If [
$s
≠ "Working" ]
# exit after 100 times, which would be about 100000 records.
Set Variable [
$counter
; Value:
$counter
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$counter
> 100 ]
End Loop

      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.Import.Todo") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$todo</Name>
  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.Import.Status") </Calculation>

functions.name: JSON.InsertOrUpdateRecord
functions.description: Inserts or updates a record in a table in one line using JSON. If the insert fails due to duplicate ID, we will update the record values. You pass to this function table name (and optional filename). To identify the record you pass the name of the ID field and the value for the record. The ID field must be unique. Then you pass a JSON with pairs of field names and values for each field you like to change in the record. This function combines JSON.InsertRecord and JSON.UpdateRecord . If the ID is already used, we update, else we insert a new record. You can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. You can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. You can use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL function to see later what SQL was created and run. The SQL functions in FileMaker do not trigger OnWindowsTransaction script trigger. If the record is locked, you get an error like this: [MBS] ERROR:  (301): Record is locked by another user. Your script may then wait and try again later. Does not take part in transactions in FileMaker. Changes are made directly to the database, so you can't revert them later with Revert Transaction script step. We auto convert text values to date, time or timestamp if the plugin sees the fields having such a type. Otherwise the type of JSON value defines the data type. If JSON contains an array or object, we make text from it. You can use FM.SQL.Execute function to query records in FileMaker and get them as JSON with FM.SQL.JSONRecords function. If needed, use JSON.ReplaceEvaluate or JSON.Replace to adjust the JSON and make modifications. Then use JSON.InsertRecord or JSON.InsertOrUpdateRecord to insert them.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: PrimaryKeys
parameters.description: The JSON with keys and values which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be RecordID for the record ID. Field name can be the result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically. If field name is a number with field ID, so we lookup name by ID.

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: The JSON object with the record.

examples.label: Insert or update record:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.InsertOrUpdateRecord"; ""; "Contacts"; 
JSONSetElement ( "{}" ; 
	GetFieldName ( Contacts::ID ) ; "123" ; JSONString ); 
JSONSetElement ( 
	JSONSetElement ( "{}" ; 
		GetFieldName ( Contacts::FirstName ); "Joe"; JSONString); 
		GetFieldName ( Contacts::TheNumber ); 125; JSONNumber)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.InsertRecord
functions.description: Inserts a new record in a table in one line using JSON. You pass to this function table name (and optional filename). Then you pass a JSON with a field names and values for each field you like to set in the new record. As we can't know the new ID assigned for the record, you can help yourself by passing in an UUID for a field and later select that to get the auto assigned primary key (or have the UUID be the primary key, so you don't need a query). See also JSON.UpdateRecord to update a record. You can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. You can use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL function to see later what SQL was created and run. The SQL functions in FileMaker do not trigger OnWindowsTransaction script trigger. Does not take part in transactions in FileMaker. Changes are made directly to the database, so you can't revert them later with Revert Transaction script step. We auto convert text values to date, time or timestamp if the plugin sees the fields having such a type. Otherwise the type of JSON value defines the data type. If JSON contains an array or object, we make text from it. You can use FM.SQL.Execute function to query records in FileMaker and get them as JSON with FM.SQL.JSONRecords function. If needed, use JSON.ReplaceEvaluate or JSON.Replace to adjust the JSON and make modifications. Then use JSON.InsertRecord or JSON.InsertOrUpdateRecord to insert them.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files. Using a table in another database file may require you adding the other file as an external data source and adding the external table to your relationship graph to grant access.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The JSON object with the record.

examples.label: Insert records with JSON:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.InsertRecord"; ""; "Contacts"; "{\"FirstName\": \"John\", \"TheNumber\": 123}")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Insert with repeating fields using []:

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.InsertRecord"; ""; "Assets"; "{\"SomeField[1]\": \"John\", \"SomeField[2]\": \"Miller\"}")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.InsertRecords
functions.description: Inserts multiple records in a table in one line using JSON. Like JSON.InsertRecord , but takes a JSON array.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files. Using a table in another database file may require you adding the other file as an external data source and adding the external table to your relationship graph to grant access.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: JSON

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.IsValid
functions.description: Checks if JSON text is valid. This parses the JSON text and returns 1 on success or 0 on failure. This checks if the given text is a valid JSON fragment and can be e.g. added as value to a JSON object or array. So this returns 1 for proper formatted boolean, number and text pieces, too.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The JSON text to parse.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.JSONPatch.ApplyPatch
functions.description: Applies a patch to a json document. The jsonpatch functions implement the IETF standard JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) Patch. The JSON Patch IETF standard requires that the JSON Patch method is atomic, so that if any JSON Patch operation results in an error, the target document is unchanged. The patch function implements this requirement by generating the inverse commands and building an undo stack, which is executed if any part of the patch fails.

parameters.name: json1
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: jsonPatch
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
json1 = "{\"people\":[{\"first\":\"Christian\",\"last\":\"Miller\",\"city\":\"Nickenich\"}]}";
json2 = "{\"people\":[{\"first\":\"Peter\",\"city\":\"Nickenich\", \"zip\": 56645 }]}";
patch =
MBS
( "
JSON.JSONPatch.FromDiff
"; json1; json2 );
json3 =
MBS
( "JSON.JSONPatch.ApplyPatch"; json1; patch )
]; json1 & ¶ & ¶ & patch & ¶ & ¶ & json3 )
Example result:
{
"people"
:[{
"first"
:
"Christian"
,
"last"
:
"Miller"
,
"city"
:
"Nickenich"
}]}

[
    {
"op"
:
"replace"
,
"path"
:
"/people/0/first"
,
"value"
:
"Peter"
}, 
    {
"op"
:
"remove"
,
"path"
:
"/people/0/last"
}, 
    {
"op"
:
"add"
,
"path"
:
"/people/0/zip"
,
"value"
:
56645
}
]

{
"people"
: [
        {
"first"
:
"Peter"
,
"city"
:
"Nickenich"
,
"zip"
:
56645
}
    ]
}
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
json1 = "{\"people\":[{\"first\":\"Christian\",\"last\":\"Miller\",\"city\":\"Nickenich\"}]}";
json2 = "{\"people\":[{\"first\":\"Peter\",\"city\":\"Nickenich\", \"zip\": 56645 }]}";
patch = MBS( "JSON.JSONPatch.FromDiff"; json1; json2 );
json3 = MBS( "JSON.JSONPatch.ApplyPatch"; json1; patch )
]; json1 & ¶ & ¶ & patch & ¶ & ¶ & json3 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.JSONPatch.FromDiff
functions.description: Create a JSON Patch from a diff of two json documents. Returns a JSON Patch. The jsonpatch functions implement the IETF standard JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) Patch. The JSON Patch IETF standard requires that the JSON Patch method is atomic, so that if any JSON Patch operation results in an error, the target document is unchanged. The patch function implements this requirement by generating the inverse commands and building an undo stack, which is executed if any part of the patch fails. Use JSON.JSONPatch.ApplyPatch to apply the patch to JSON data later.

parameters.name: json1
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: json2
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

functions.name: JSON.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of current JSON objects. Returns IDs for all JSON objects in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call JSON.Release in your scripts. Normally after all scripts are done, this list should be empty.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.Merge
functions.description: Merges multiple JSON objects or arrays. You can pass more than 2 parameters if you need to merge multiple objects or arrays. For arrays, we check if the value is already there and if not, we add it. For objects, we check the values for each key. If it doesn't exist, we copy it over. If it exists, we check arrays and objects again, but other values just get replaced. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat json2 parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: json1
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: json2...
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.Parse
functions.description: Parses JSON text and return reference number. Please use JSON.Release later to free the json object. You can pass the reference number to all MBS JSON functions instead of JSON text.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The JSON text to parse.

examples.label: Parse an array:
examples.text_code:
# parse some json
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value:
MBS
( "JSON.Parse"; "[1,2,3]" )]
# query array size:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.GetArraySize
";
$ref
)]
# free memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.Release
";
$ref
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> parse some json</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Parse"; "[1,2,3]" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ref</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> query array size:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.GetArraySize"; $ref)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Release"; $ref)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Parse some JSON, display it formatted and release:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "JSON.Parse"; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}" )]
Show Custom Dialog [
$json
;
MBS
("
JSON.Format
";
$json
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.Release
";
$json
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Parse"; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>$json; MBS("JSON.Format"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$json)</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Release"; $json)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query IP via ipinfo.io:
examples.text_code:
Go to Layout [“Table”]
New Record/Request
#Start new session
Set Variable [
$curl
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.New
")]
#Set URL to load (HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, FTPS, SFTP, etc.)
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.SetOptionURL
";
$curl
; "http://ipinfo.io")]
#RUN now
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Perform
";
$curl
)]
#Check result
Set Field [
Table::DebugLog
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
)]
Set Field [
Table::Result
;
MBS
("
CURL.GetDebugMessages
";
$curl
; "UTF8")]
If [
$result
= "OK"]
#Check HTTP error code
Set Variable [
$response
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.GetResponseCode
";
$curl
)]
If [
$response
= 200]
#Query values from JSON
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "JSON.Parse";
Table::Result
)]
Set Field [
Table::IP
;
MBS
("
JSON.GetPathItem
";
$json
; "ip"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::Hostname
;
MBS
("
JSON.GetPathItem
";
$json
; "hostname"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::City
;
MBS
("
JSON.GetPathItem
";
$json
; "city"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::Region
;
MBS
("
JSON.GetPathItem
";
$json
; "region"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::Country
;
MBS
("
JSON.GetPathItem
";
$json
; "country"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::Location
;
MBS
("
JSON.GetPathItem
";
$json
; "loc"; 1)]
Set Field [
Table::Company
or Provider;
MBS
("
JSON.GetPathItem
";
$json
; "org"; 1)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.Release
";
$json
)]
End If
End If
#Cleanup
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
CURL.Release
";
$curl
)]
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="Table"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Start new session</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$curl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Set URL to load (HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, FTPS, SFTP, etc.)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.SetOptionURL"; $curl; "http://ipinfo.io")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>RUN now</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Perform"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Check result</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="DebugLog"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetDebugMessages"; $curl; "UTF8")</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$result = "OK"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Check HTTP error code</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.GetResponseCode"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$response</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$response = 200</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Query values from JSON</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Parse"; Table::Result )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "ip"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="IP"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "hostname"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Hostname"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem";  $json; "city"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="City"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "region"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Region"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "country"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Country"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "loc"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Location"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.GetPathItem"; $json; "org"; 1)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="Company or Provider"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Release"; $json )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CURL.Release"; $curl)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Work with huge JSON by parsing once:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.json" ]
# read all the text from disk
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.ReadTextFile
";
$path
; "UTF-8") ]
# now parse JSON
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.Parse";
$text
) ]
# and do something with it
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.GetArraySize
";
$json
) ]
# when done, please free memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.Release
";
$json
) ]

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

functions.name: JSON.Query
functions.description: Performs a JSON Path query. Evaluates the root value against the JSONPath expression and returns an array of values or normalized path expressions. Returns a JSON with an array containing either values or normalized path expressions matching the JSONPath expression, or an empty array if there is no match. To learn more about JSONPath, please check this website: https://goessner.net/articles/JsonPath/ Learn more about the JSONPath implementation here: https://danielaparker.github.io/JsonCons.Net/articles/JsonPath/JsonConsJsonPath.html See also JSON.Replace to replace found values with new values.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The query string.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags.

examples.label: Query input:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>let([
  json = "{\"test\":123}";
  result = MBS("JSON.Query"; json; "$"; 0)
]; result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query with regular expression:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>let([
json = "{
    \"store\": {
        \"book\": [
            {
                \"category\": \"reference\", 
                \"author\": \"Nigel Rees\", 
                \"title\": \"Sayings of the Century\", 
                \"price\": 8.95
            }, 
            {
                \"category\": \"fiction\", 
                \"author\": \"Evelyn Waugh\", 
                \"title\": \"Sword of Honour\", 
                \"price\": 12.99
            }, 
            {
                \"category\": \"fiction\", 
                \"author\": \"Herman Melville\", 
                \"title\": \"Moby Dick\", 
                \"isbn\": \"0-553-21311-3\", 
                \"price\": 8.99
            }, 
            {
                \"category\": \"fiction\", 
                \"author\": \"J. R. R. Tolkien\", 
                \"title\": \"The Lord of the Rings\", 
                \"isbn\": \"0-395-19395-8\", 
                \"price\": 22.99
            }
        ], 
        \"bicycle\": {
            \"color\": \"red\", 
            \"price\": 19.95
        }
    }
}";
// All books whose author's name starts with Evelyn
result = MBS("JSON.Query"; json; "$.store.book[?(@.author =~ /Evelyn.*?/)]"; 0)
]; result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query all keys:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>let([
json = "{
    \"store\": {
        \"book\": [
            {
                \"category\": \"reference\", 
                \"author\": \"Nigel Rees\", 
                \"title\": \"Sayings of the Century\", 
                \"price\": 8.95
            }, 
            {
                \"category\": \"fiction\", 
                \"author\": \"Evelyn Waugh\", 
                \"title\": \"Sword of Honour\", 
                \"price\": 12.99
            }, 
            {
                \"category\": \"fiction\", 
                \"author\": \"Herman Melville\", 
                \"title\": \"Moby Dick\", 
                \"isbn\": \"0-553-21311-3\", 
                \"price\": 8.99
            }, 
            {
                \"category\": \"fiction\", 
                \"author\": \"J. R. R. Tolkien\", 
                \"title\": \"The Lord of the Rings\", 
                \"isbn\": \"0-395-19395-8\", 
                \"price\": 22.99
            }
        ], 
        \"bicycle\": {
            \"color\": \"red\", 
            \"price\": 19.95
        }
    }
}";
// All keys in the second book
result = MBS("JSON.Query"; json; "keys($.store.book[1])[*]"; 0)
]; result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find all books matching a criteria and return list of matching title values:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>let([
json = "{
    \"store\": {
        \"book\": [
            {
                \"category\": \"reference\", 
                \"author\": \"Nigel Rees\", 
                \"title\": \"Sayings of the Century\", 
                \"price\": 8.95
            }, 
            {
                \"category\": \"fiction\", 
                \"author\": \"Evelyn Waugh\", 
                \"title\": \"Sword of Honour\", 
                \"price\": 12.99
            }, 
            {
                \"category\": \"fiction\", 
                \"author\": \"Herman Melville\", 
                \"title\": \"Moby Dick\", 
                \"isbn\": \"0-553-21311-3\", 
                \"price\": 8.99
            }, 
            {
                \"category\": \"fiction\", 
                \"author\": \"J. R. R. Tolkien\", 
                \"title\": \"The Lord of the Rings\", 
                \"isbn\": \"0-395-19395-8\", 
                \"price\": 22.99
            }
        ], 
        \"bicycle\": {
            \"color\": \"red\", 
            \"price\": 19.95
        }
    }
}";
// Find all book titles with price > 10
result = MBS("JSON.Query"; json; "$.store[@.book[?(@.price > 10)].title]"; 0)
]; result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find matching entry in JSON Array of Objects:

examples.label: Find matching entry in JSON Array of Objects with two criterias:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Query";
"[ {\"test\":\"abc\", \"ID\": 1}, {\"test\":\"Hello\", \"ID\": 2} ]";
"$[?(@.test == 'Hello' || @.test == 'abc')]" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.Release
functions.description: Frees a json reference number. Such reference numbers are returned by JSON.Parse , JSON.CreateArrayRef , JSON.CreateObjectRef , JSON.GetArrayItem , JSON.GetObjectItem , JSON.GetObjectItemTreeRef or JSON.SetVariables .

parameters.name: JSONRef
parameters.description: The JSON reference number.

examples.label: Parse some JSON, display it formatted and release:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.Parse
"; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}" )]
Show Custom Dialog [
$json
;
MBS
("
JSON.Format
";
$json
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "JSON.Release";
$json
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Parse"; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>$json; MBS("JSON.Format"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$json)</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Release"; $json)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Releases all JSON objects. After this call all reference numbers are invalid. This is useful if you start a script which creates dozens of JSON reference numbers and you don't want to keep track of those IDs. Be aware that on a server this may free objects, which are used by other scripts running in parallel!

examples.label: Release all JSON objects:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.ReleaseAll")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.Replace
functions.description: Searches for all values that match the JSONPath expression and replaces them with the specified value. Returns an error if JSONPath evaluation fails. See also JSON.Query to just search without replace.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to query.

parameters.name: ReplaceJSON
parameters.description: The new JSON value.

examples.label: Find an entry in the books array and change the value:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
json = "{
    \"books\": [
        {
            \"author\": \"Haruki Murakami\",
            \"category\": \"fiction\",
            \"price\": 20.0,
            \"title\": \"A Wild Sheep Chase\"
        },
        {
            \"author\": \"Sergei Lukyanenko\",
            \"category\": \"fiction\",
            \"price\": 23.58,
            \"title\": \"The Night Watch\"
        },
        {
            \"author\": \"Graham Greene\",
            \"category\": \"fiction\",
            \"price\": 21.99,
            \"title\": \"The Comedians\"
        },
        {
            \"author\": \"Phillips, David Atlee\",
            \"category\": \"memoir\",
            \"title\": \"The Night Watch\"
        }
    ]
}";
output = MBS( "JSON.Replace"; json; "$.books[?(@.title == 'A Wild Sheep Chase')].price"; 123 )
]; output)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Replace null values in object with empty quotes:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "JSON.Replace"; "{
\"author\": \"test\",
\"category\": null,
\"price\": null,
\"title\": \"test\"
}"; "$..[?(@ == null)]"; "\"\"" )
Example result:
{
"author"
:
"test"
,
"category"
:
""
,
"price"
:
""
,
"title"
:
"test"
}

\"author\": \"test\",
\"category\": null,
\"price\": null,
\"title\": \"test\"
}"; "$..[?(@ == null)]"; "\"\"" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Change colors in styled text:
examples.text_code:
# styled text to JSON
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
("
Text.Styles
";
Test::Input
) ]
#  # change all red to 123
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.Replace";
$json
; "$..red"; 123) ]
#  # change all green to 0
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.Replace";
$json
; "$..green"; 0) ]
#  # convert back to styled text
Set Field [
Test::Output
;
MBS
("
Text.FromStyles
";
$json
) ]
examples.xml_code:

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  # change all green to 0</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.Replace"; $json; "$..green"; 0) </Calculation>

functions.name: JSON.ReplaceEvaluate
functions.description: Searches for all values that match the JSONPath expression and replaces them with the result of the evaluation. Returns an error if JSONPath evaluation fails. See also JSON.Query to just search without replace and JSON.Replace to replace without expression. If the expression is not compiling, we return an error. Your expression should return the new JSON to replace the found item. But you can also return empty text to skip this.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to query.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to run. You may call JSON.CurrentMatch function to get current JSON as well as JSON.CurrentMatchPath function to get the path.

examples.label: Pass through current match:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.ReplaceEvaluate"; JSON::JSON1; "$[?(@.PrimaryKey)]"; "Let([json = MBS(\"JSON.CurrentMatch\")]; json")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Replace all found records with 123:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.ReplaceEvaluate"; JSON::JSON1; "$[?(@.PrimaryKey)]"; "123")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$list = "";

examples.label: Collect primary keys we find on the way:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
$list = "";
r = MBS("JSON.ReplaceEvaluate"; JSON::JSON1; "$[?(@.PrimaryKey)]"; "Let([
json = MBS(\"JSON.CurrentMatch\"); 
key = MBS( \"JSON.GetPathItem\"; json; \"PrimaryKey\"; 1 ); 
$list = $list & key & \¶

// Normally you do this with JSON.Query function more efficiently!</Calculation>

examples.label: Now search objects with PrimaryKey and add entries for Assignments:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.ReplaceEvaluate"; JSON::JSON1; "$[?(@.PrimaryKey)]"; "Let([
/*the current match */ 
$json = MBS(\"JSON.CurrentMatch\"); 
/* now lookup primary key inside it */ 
$key = MBS( \"JSON.GetPathItem\"; $json; \"PrimaryKey\"; 1 ); 
/* look for related records */ 
$sql = MBS( \"FM.SQL.Execute\"; Get(FileName); \"SELECT Name, Note, PrimaryKey, EmployeeForeignKey, \\\"Date Returned\\\" FROM Assignments WHERE AssetForeignKey = ?\"; $key);
/* now get a JSON array for the found set */ 
foundJSON = MBS( \"FM.SQL.JSONRecords\"; $sql; \"Name\¶Note\¶AssignmentsPrimaryKey\¶EmployeeForeignKey\¶Date Returned\" );
/* and cleanup memory */ 
$r = MBS(\"FM.SQL.Release\"; $sql);
/* build new JSON with the related records added */ 
$newjson = MBS(\"JSON.AddItemToObject\"; $json; \"Assignments\"; foundJSON )
]; $newjson)")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Now search objects with PrimaryKey and add entries for Employees on the new Assignments:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.ReplaceEvaluate"; JSON::JSON3; "$[*].Assignments[?@.AssignmentsPrimaryKey]"; "Let([
/*the current match */ 
$json = MBS(\"JSON.CurrentMatch\"); 
/* now lookup primary key inside it */ 
$key = MBS( \"JSON.GetPathItem\"; $json; \"EmployeeForeignKey\"; 1 ); 
/* look for related records */ 
$sql = MBS( \"FM.SQL.Execute\"; Get(FileName); \"SELECT Name, PrimaryKey, \\\"First Name\\\" FROM Employees WHERE PrimaryKey = ?\"; $key);
/* now get a JSON array for the found set */ 
foundJSON = MBS( \"FM.SQL.JSONRecords\"; $sql; \"Name\¶PrimaryKey\¶First Name\" );
/* and cleanup memory */ 
$r = MBS(\"FM.SQL.Release\"; $sql);
/* build new JSON with the related records added */ 
$newjson = MBS(\"JSON.AddItemToObject\"; $json; \"Employees\"; foundJSON )
]; $newjson)")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.Search
functions.description: Performs a JMESPath query. The jmespath extension implements JMESPath. JMESPath is a query language for transforming JSON documents into other JSON documents. It's supported in both the AWS and Azure CLI and has libraries available in a number of languages. To learn more about JMESPath, please check this website: https://jmespath.org See also JSON.Query for queries with JSONPath expressions.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to query.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.Search"; "{ \"locations\": [
 {\"name\": \"Seattle\", \"state\": \"WA\"},
 {\"name\": \"New York\", \"state\": \"NY\"},
 {\"name\": \"Bellevue\", \"state\": \"WA\"},
 {\"name\": \"Olympia\", \"state\": \"WA\"}
]}"; "locations[?state == 'WA'].name | sort(@) | {WashingtonCities: join(', ', @)}")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.SetFormatOptions
functions.description: Sets options for formatting JSON. If called without parameters, it will reset to default, Char(9) for indention and a space before object values. For use in the JSON.Format function.

parameters.name: tabIndention
parameters.description: What to use for indention. e.g. Char(9) for tabs, Char(32) for spaces.

parameters.name: tabAfterObjectKey
parameters.description: What to use for space between the double colon and the value in an object. By default this is a space character.

examples.label: Try variants:
examples.text_code:
Let(
[
json = "{\"test\":1234, \"values\":[2,3,4]}";
// default settings
format1 =
MBS
( "
JSON.Format
"; json );
// use minus and plus, so you see it:
r =
MBS
( "JSON.SetFormatOptions"; "-"; "+" );
format2 =
MBS
( "
JSON.Format
"; json );
// no tabs
r =
MBS
( "JSON.SetFormatOptions"; ""; "" );
format3 =
MBS
( "
JSON.Format
"; json );
// reset to default settings
r =
MBS
( "JSON.SetFormatOptions" )
];
format1 & ¶ & format2 & ¶ & format3
)
Example result:
{
"test"
:
1234
,
"values"
: [
2
,
3
,
4
]
}
{
-
"test"
:+
1234
,
-
"values"
:+[
2
,
3
,
4
]
}
{
"test"
:
1234
,
"values"
:[
2
,
3
,
4
]
}
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
	[
		json = "{\"test\":1234, \"values\":[2,3,4]}";
// default settings
		format1 = MBS( "JSON.Format"; json );
// use minus and plus, so you see it:
		r = MBS( "JSON.SetFormatOptions"; "-"; "+" );
		format2 = MBS( "JSON.Format"; json );
// no tabs
		r = MBS( "JSON.SetFormatOptions"; ""; "" );
		format3 = MBS( "JSON.Format"; json );
// reset to default settings
		r = MBS( "JSON.SetFormatOptions" )
	];
	format1 & ¶ & format2 & ¶ & format3
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.SetPathItem
functions.description: Sets a value in a JSON hierarchy. The path describes the way through the hierarchy to find the JSON node to return. Please pass in new value as JSON. Version 9.2 automatically creates missing object/array entries. The value parameter will be treated as text if it is not a valid JSON instead of reporting an error. Implemented FileMaker syntax in v12.4 for JSON element get/set like in JSONSetElement function, so you can use dot notation, [array index] or quoted text in brackets.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path through the json hierarchy as list. For object, pass name of item to choose. For array pass index. For last array you can use special value "append" to add an entry.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value as JSON.

functions.name: JSON.SetVariables
functions.description: Creates local variables in script for JSON. Returns list of variable names. If variables receive multiple values the name in the result comes the highest index in square brackets. Recognizes numbers and passes them through as numbers. Arrays and objects in JSON are passed as JSON. Null values make empty variables and boolean values are passed as 1 for true and 0 for false. You can call JSON.ClearVariables later to clear those variables, but at the end of the script all variables will go away. Variable names are shortened to a maximum of 100 characters.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference. Must be an object.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to use for the variables names.

examples.label: Set some variables using JSON:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Variables
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.SetVariables";
Table::JSON
Insert) ]
# @variable
$FirstName
,
$LastName
,
$Birthday
Show Custom Dialog [ "Names" ;
$FirstName
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.ClearVariables
";
$Variables
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.SetVariables"; Table::JSON Insert) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Variables</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> @variable $FirstName, $LastName, $Birthday</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Names" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$FirstName </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.ClearVariables"; $Variables) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Test variables in JSON and XML:
examples.text_code: # our test text with some variable values containing backslash, quote and a new line Set Variable [ $xml ; Value: "Hello¶World\¶\t" & Char(34) & "!" ] Set Variable [ $json ; Value: JSONSetElement ( "{}" ; "abc" ; "Hello¶World\¶\a" & Char(34) & "!" ; JSONString ) ] # # set variables Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" XML.SetVariables "; $xml ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("JSON.SetVariables"; $json ) ] # declare for our variable check # @param $test , $abc # # and display Show Custom Dialog [ "XML" ; $test ] Show Custom Dialog [ "JSON" ; $abc ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> our test text with some variable values containing backslash, quote and a new line</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"<a><test>Hello¶World\¶\t" & Char(34) & "!</test></a>" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$xml</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>JSONSetElement ( "{}" ; "abc" ; "Hello¶World\¶\a" & Char(34) & "!"  ; JSONString ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set variables</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("XML.SetVariables"; $xml) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.SetVariables"; $json) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> declare for our variable check</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> @param $test, $abc</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and display</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "XML" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$test </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "JSON" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$abc </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.description: Sorts the JSON array/object. Use JSON.SortWithEvaluate to sort entries within an array of objects.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: JSON.SortWithEvaluate
functions.description: Sorts the JSON array/object using an expression to evaluate. The expression is run every time two values are compared, so keep it quick. It should return 1 if leftJSON is smaller than rightJSON. Our normal JSON.Sort is faster due to not having the overhead of evaluating expressions.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can include "leftJSON", "rightJSON" as variables. You can call other MBS JSON functions if needed.

examples.label: Sort with evaluate:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "JSON.SortWithEvaluate"; "[2,9,3,1]"; "
MBS
( \"
JSON.GetValue
\"; leftJSON) <
MBS
( \"
JSON.GetValue
\"; rightJSON)" )
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
