functions.name: JSON.ToCSV
functions.description: Converts JSON to CSV. You may prepare the JSON to be an array of objects using JSON.Query , JSON.Search or one of the other JSON functions. Please use Text.WriteTextFile to write to text file if you need CSV export.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags. 1: Pass 1 to put all text values in quotes. 2: Skip column names in first row.

parameters.name: rowSeparator
parameters.description: The row separator. Default is CR. Can be more than one character.

parameters.name: colSeparator
parameters.description: The column separator. Default is semicolon. Can be more than one character.

examples.label: Convert to CSV:

MBS
( "JSON.ToCSV"; "[
{\"name\": \"Seattle\", \"state\": \"WA\"},
{\"name\": \"New York\", \"state\": \"NY\"},
{\"name\": \"Bellevue\", \"state\": \"WA\"},
{\"name\": \"Olympia\", \"state\": \"WA\"}
]")

functions.name: JSON.ToHTML
functions.description: Converts JSON to HTML. We build for you a HTML with tables for each array and object. We include values and tag rows with even/odd CSS classes.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for processing. Currently you can pass 1 to exclude html header and just get a HTML table. Add 2 to process embedded quoted JSON (new in v14.2).

parameters.name: CSS Style

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.ToHTML"; JSON Input; 0; 
/* CSS style to include */
"<style>
td
{
	vertical-align:top;
}

.odd
{
	background-color: white;
}

.even
{
	background-color: #DDD;
}
</style>")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: A JSON text or reference.

examples.label: Creates a simple toon:

parameters.name: JSON

functions.name: JSON.Unflatten
functions.description: Unflattens that object back to the original json. The keys in the flattened object are normalized json paths. The values are primitive (string, number, boolean, or null), empty object ({}) or empty array ([]).

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.UpdateRecord
functions.description: Update an existing record in a table in one line using JSON. You pass to this function table name (and optional filename). To identify the record you pass the name of the ID field and the value for the record. The ID field must be unique. Then you pass a JSON with pairs of field names and values for each field you like to change in the record. Please note that JSON.UpdateRecord and JSON.UpdateRecords are nearly identical. Just JSON.UpdateRecord provides the check to make sure only one record matches while JSON.UpdateRecords allows several records to be updated. So with JSON.UpdateRecords you can easily erase a lot of data if you pass wrong ID! You can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. You can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. You can use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL function to see later what SQL was created and run. The SQL functions in FileMaker do not trigger OnWindowsTransaction script trigger. If the record is locked, you get an error like this: [MBS] ERROR:  (301): Record is locked by another user. Your script may then wait and try again later. Does not take part in transactions in FileMaker. Changes are made directly to the database, so you can't revert them later with Revert Transaction script step. We auto convert text values to date, time or timestamp if the plugin sees the fields having such a type. Otherwise the type of JSON value defines the data type. If JSON contains an array or object, we make text from it.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files. Using a table in another database file may require you adding the other file as an external data source and adding the external table to your relationship graph to grant access.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: PrimaryKeys
parameters.description: The JSON with keys and values which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ROWID for the record ID. Field name can be the result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically. If field name is a number with field ID, so we lookup name by ID.

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: The JSON object with the record.

examples.label: Update a record with JSON:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.UpdateRecord"; ""; "Contacts"; "{\"PrimaryKey\": " &  Contacts::PrimaryKey & "}"; "{\"FirstName\": \"John\", \"TheNumber\": 125}")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Update a record and build JSON on the fly:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.UpdateRecord"; ""; "Contacts"; 
JSONSetElement ( "{}" ; 
	GetFieldName ( Contacts::PrimaryKey ) ; Contacts::PrimaryKey ; JSONString ); 
JSONSetElement ( 
	JSONSetElement ( "{}" ; 
		GetFieldName ( Contacts::FirstName ); "John"; JSONString); 
		GetFieldName ( Contacts::TheNumber ); 134; JSONNumber)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JSON.UpdateRecords
functions.description: Updates existing records in a table in one line using JSON. You pass to this function table name (and optional filename). To identify the record you pass the name of the ID field and the value for the record. The ID field must not be unique, so several records can be updated at once. Then you pass one pair of field name and value for each field you like to set in the new record. Please note that JSON.UpdateRecord and JSON.UpdateRecords are nearly identical. Just JSON.UpdateRecord provides the check to make sure only one record matches while JSON.UpdateRecords allows several records to be updated. So with JSON.UpdateRecords you can easily erase a lot of data if you pass wrong ID! You can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. You can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. You can use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL function to see later what SQL was created and run.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: PrimaryKeys
parameters.description: The JSON with keys and values which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ROWID for the record ID. Field name can be the result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically. If field name is a number with field ID, so we lookup name by ID.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The JSON object with the record.

examples.label: Find records with matching first name and update to new last name:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("JSON.UpdateRecords"; ""; "Contacts"; "{\"First Name\": \"Joe\"}"; "{\"Last Name\": \"Miller\"}") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ Title: "Failed to update."; Message:
$r
; Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes” ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.UpdateRecords"; ""; "Contacts"; "{\"First Name\": \"Joe\"}"; "{\"Last Name\": \"Miller\"}") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> Title: "Failed to update."; Message: $r</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes” </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ListDialog.AddItemToList

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: The tag, e.g. the record ID.

MBS( "ListDialog.AddItemToList"; "Bob"; 1 )
MBS( "ListDialog.AddItemToList"; "Joe"; 2 )
MBS( "ListDialog.AddItemToList"; "Tim"; 3 )</Calculation>

functions.name: ListDialog.AddItemsToList

parameters.name: tags

$r

functions.name: ListDialog.AddSQL
functions.description: Adds items via SQL statement. First field from SQL result is used as text, second as tag and third as header flag. Tag and header columns are optional. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database.

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Optional, pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

functions.result: Returns number of items added or error.

examples.label: Add items:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("ListDialog.AddSQL"; "select Text, Tag, Header from Liste")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.AddSQL"; "select Text, Tag, Header from Liste")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add value list entries for texts and tags:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("ListDialog.AddSQL"; "SELECT DISTINCT ValueList2, ValueList1 FROM Kontakte WHERE Length(ValueList2) > 0 ORDER BY ValueList2 ASC")]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.AddSQL"; "SELECT DISTINCT ValueList2, ValueList1 FROM Kontakte WHERE Length(ValueList2) > 0 ORDER BY ValueList2 ASC")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

( "ListDialog.ClearList" )
MBS
( "
ListDialog.AddItemToList
"; "Bob"; 1 )

( "
ListDialog.AddItemToList
"; "Joe"; 2 )

( "
ListDialog.AddItemToList
"; "Tim"; 3 )

MBS( "ListDialog.AddItemToList"; "Bob"; 1 )
MBS( "ListDialog.AddItemToList"; "Joe"; 2 )
MBS( "ListDialog.AddItemToList"; "Tim"; 3 )</Calculation>

    </Repetition>

</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate.

parameters.name: ButtonIndex
parameters.description: The index for the other button from 0 to 2.

$r
; Value:

("
ListDialog.SetOtherButtonLabel

$r
; Value:

("ListDialog.SetOtherButtonEvaluate"; "
MBS
( \"
ShowURL
\"; \"https://mbsplugins.eu\" )") ]

    </Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: ListDialog.SetShowCheckboxes
functions.description: Sets whether to show checkboxes. If checkboxes are enabled, we show a checkbox in each row. You can set check states with ListDialog.SetChecked and query them later with ListDialog.GetChecked . The Select button is enabled, if at least one checkbox is marked or empty selection is allowed. Users can filter and rows remember their checked state even when not visible.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Combination of various flags: 0: hide checkboxes 1: show checkboxes 2: show alternative style on Windows 4: limit to one checkbox being checked (new in v14.3)

examples.label: Enable checkboxes:
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ListDialog.ShowDialog
functions.description: Shows the dialog. Returns the OK, Other, or Cancel to indicate which button was used to close the dialog. Due to a bug in 15.2, it returns "Select" unless changed with ListDialog.SetSelectButtonValue function. Added iOS support in version 15.3.

parameters.name: TopMost
parameters.description: Whether the dialog will get top most flag on Windows. Pass 1 to make it topmost, pass 0 or nothing for normal behavior.

functions.result: Returns OK, Other, Cancel or error.

examples.label: Shows a dialog:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.ClearList
")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.SetPrompt
"; "Please select an item:")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.AddItemToList
"; "First Item"; "1234")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.SetAllowMultipleSelection
"; 1)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ListDialog.SetSelectedTitle
"; "1234")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("ListDialog.ShowDialog")]
Set Field [
ListDialog::Result
;
$r
]
Set Field [
ListDialog::Selected
Tag;
MBS
("
ListDialog.GetSelectedTag
")]
Set Field [
ListDialog::Selected
;
MBS
("
ListDialog.GetSelectedTitle
")]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.ClearList")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.SetPrompt"; "Please select an item:")</Calculation>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ListDialog.AddItemToList"; "First Item"; "1234")</Calculation>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>

functions.name: ListDialog.Sort
functions.description: Sorts entries. Sorts ignoring case, by default ascending.

parameters.name: Descending
parameters.description: Pass 1 for descending sort. Default is 0 for ascending.

functions.name: Phidget.AddPositionOffset
functions.description: Adds an offset (positive or negative) to the current position. Please check phidgets documentation about what values are acceptable for your device. This can be especially useful for zeroing position. For Stepper, MotorPositionController, or BLDCMotor only. This function is free to use. Created 16th August 2023 , last changed 16th August 2023 Path.ShortPath - Phidget.ChildDevices 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: positionOffset
parameters.description: Amount to offset the position by

functions.name: Phidget.ChildDevices
functions.description: Queries child devices for a device. Returns a list of Phidget reference numbers. This function checks for a license. Created 16th August 2023 , last changed 16th August 2023 Phidget.AddPositionOffset - Phidget.Clear 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

functions.name: Phidget.Clear
functions.description: Clears all pixels in the current frame buffer. Changes made to the frame buffer must be flushed to the LCD screen using Phidget.Flush .

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

functions.name: Phidget.ClearLEDs
functions.description: Turn off all LEDs. Any active animations will be stopped. Only for LED arrays.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

functions.name: Phidget.ClearScriptTrigger
functions.description: Removes a script trigger for a phidget. You may not need to do this as closing the phidget will stop triggers. See Phidget.SetScriptTrigger for the list of events.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: Event
parameters.description: The name of the event.

functions.name: Phidget.Close
functions.description: Closes a Phidget channel that has been opened. This function will release the channel on the Phidget device, and should be called prior to Phidget.Release .

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

examples.label: Closes the device and free memory:
examples.text_code:
If [ Length (
$$phidget
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Phidget.Close";
$$phidget
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Phidget.Release
";
$$phidget
) ]
Set Variable [
$$phidget
; Value: "" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ Length ( $$phidget ) > 0 ] 
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS("Phidget.Close"; $$phidget) ] 
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS("Phidget.Release"; $$phidget) ] 
	Set Variable [ $$phidget ; Value: "" ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Phidget.Create
functions.description: Creates an instance of a Phidget channel. Once created the channel may need to be configured to match the physical device and channel index you want to attach to, and then be opened by calling Phidget.Open . The Phidget channel handle must be released with Phidget.Release .

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type of device object to create. Can be one of VoltageRatioInput, DigitalInput, DigitalOutput, RCServo, VoltageOutput, VoltageInput, CapacitiveTouch, RFID, GPS, Gyroscope, Magnetometer, Spatial, TemperatureSensor, Encoder, FrequencyCounter, IR, PHSensor, DCMotor, CurrentInput, Stepper, LCD, MotorPositionController, BLDCMotor, DistanceSensor, LightSensor, PressureSensor, ResistanceInput, PowerGuard, SoundSensor, Hub, Dictionary, Accelerometer, HumiditySensor, LEDArray, CurrentOutput.

examples.label: Creates a LCD object:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Phidget.Create"; "LCD" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$phidget</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Phidget.DisableServerDiscovery
functions.description: Disables the dynamic discovery of servers that publish their identity. Phidget.DisableServerDiscovery does not disconnect already established connections.

parameters.name: ServerType
parameters.description: Pass none, deviceListener, Device, DeviceRemote, WWWListener, WWW, WWWRemote or SBC.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Phidget.EnableFailsafe
functions.description: Enables the failsafe feature for the channel, with a given failsafe time. The failsafe feature is intended for use in applications where it is important for the channel to enter a known safe state if the program controlling it locks up or crashes. If you do not enable the failsafe feature, the channel will carry out whatever instructions it was last given until it is explicitly told to stop. Enabling the failsafe feature starts a recurring failsafe timer for the channel. Once the failsafe timer is enabled, it must be reset within the specified time or the channel will enter a failsafe state. The failsafe timer may be reset by sending any valid command to the device*. Resetting the failsafe timer will reload the timer with the specified failsafe time, starting when the message to reset the timer is received by the Phidget. *(get requests do not typically send commands and won't reset the failsafe timer) For example: if the failsafe is enabled with a failsafe time of 1000ms, you will have 1000ms to reset the failsafe timer. Every time the failsafe timer is reset, you will have 1000ms from that time to reset the failsafe again. If the failsafe timer is not reset before it runs out, the channel will enter a failsafe state. For DC Motor channels, this will disengage the motor. Once the channel enters the failsafe state, it will reject any further input until the channel is reopened. To prevent the channel from falsely entering the failsafe state, we recommend resetting the failsafe timer as frequently as is practical for your application. A good rule of thumb is to not let more than a third of the failsafe time pass before resetting the timer. Once the failsafe timer has been set, it cannot be disabled by any means other than closing and reopening the channel. For DCMotor, DigitalOutput, PowerGuard, RCServo, Stepper, VoltageOutput, PositionController, or BLDCMotor only.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: failsafeTime
parameters.description: Failsafe timeout in milliseconds.

functions.name: Phidget.EnableServerDiscovery
functions.description: Enables the dynamic discovery of servers that publish their identity to the network. Currently Multicast DNS is used to discover and publish Phidget servers. To connect to remote Phidgets, call this function with server type DeviceRemote. Phidget.EnableServerDiscovery must be called once for each server type your program requires. Multiple calls for the same server type are ignored This call will fail with the unsupported error if your computer does not have the required mDNS support. We recommend using Bonjour Print Services on Windows and Mac, or Avahi on Linux.

parameters.name: ServerType
parameters.description: Pass none, deviceListener, Device, DeviceRemote, WWWListener, WWW, WWWRemote or SBC.

examples.label: Watch for remote devices:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Phidget.EnableServerDiscovery"; "DeviceRemote" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

MBS

$$phidget

$$phidget

functions.name: Phidget.Load
functions.description: Loads the Phidget library. You need to install client software and provide path to the DLL for Windows, framework for macOS or so file for Linux. On Windows you may need to call Process.SetDllDirectory function to tell Windows where to find dependencies for the python DLL. On Linux if you get the error wrong ELF class: ELFCLASS32 if you try to load a 32-bit library into a 64-bit application. You may also get errors about version GLIBC_2.25 or similar if your libc library on the Linux installation isn't new enough for the loaded library.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the library file.

examples.label: Load library on macOS:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Phidget.Load"; "/Library/Frameworks/Phidget22.framework") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Load library on Windows:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Phidget.Load"; "C:\\Program Files\\Phidgets\\Phidget22\\phidget22.dll") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Load library on Linux:

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Phidget.Open
functions.description: Opens the Phidget channel. The open function will return immediately, with the attachment process proceeding asynchronously. Use the Attach event or Attached property to determine when the channel is ready to use. But if you pass a timeout value. the function waits a defined amount of time for the device to attach. The specific channel to be opened can be specified by setting any of the following properties: DeviceSerialNumber DeviceLabel Channel HubPort IsHubPortDevice ServerName IsLocal IsRemote Use Phidget.SetProperty to set these properties.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: TimeOut
parameters.description: The timeout for waiting for the device to respond. Default is 5000 milliseconds.

examples.label: Open a phidget:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$phidget
; Value:
MBS
( "
Phidget.Create
"; "LCD" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Phidget.Open";
$$phidget
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Phidget.Create"; "LCD" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$phidget</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Phidget.Open"; $$phidget ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Phidget.Release
functions.description: Frees memory for a Phidget object.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget. Or a list of reference numbers.

examples.label: Closes the device and free memory:
examples.text_code:
If [ Length (
$$phidget
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Phidget.Close
";
$$phidget
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Phidget.Release";
$$phidget
) ]
Set Variable [
$$phidget
; Value: "" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ Length ( $$phidget ) > 0 ] 
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS("Phidget.Close"; $$phidget) ] 
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS("Phidget.Release"; $$phidget) ] 
	Set Variable [ $$phidget ; Value: "" ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Phidget.ResetFailsafe
functions.description: Resets the failsafe timer. Resets the failsafe timer, if one has been set. See Phidget.EnableFailsafe for details. This function will fail if no failsafe timer has been set for the channel.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

functions.name: Phidget.SetAnimation
functions.signature: MBS( "Phidget.SetAnimation"; PhidgetRef; animationID; pattern; StartAddress; EndAddress; Time; AnimationType )
functions.description: Display an animation. Only for LED arrays.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: animationID
parameters.description: The ID of the animation.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: The list with colors. 4 values per color with red, green, blue and white.

parameters.name: StartAddress
parameters.description: Which LED to start the animation from.

parameters.name: EndAddress
parameters.description: Which LED the animation will end on.

parameters.name: Time
parameters.description: Time between changes in ms.

parameters.name: AnimationType
parameters.description: The animation type. Can be ForwardScroll, ReverseScroll, Randomize, ForwardScrollMirror or ReverseScrollMirror.

functions.name: Phidget.SetLED
functions.signature: MBS( "Phidget.SetLED"; PhidgetRef; address; red; green; blue; white; fadeTime )
functions.description: Set the color of a single of LED. Only for LED arrays.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: The address of the LED.

parameters.name: red
parameters.description: The red value. 0 to 255.

parameters.name: green
parameters.description: The green value. 0 to 255.

parameters.name: blue
parameters.description: The blue value. 0 to 255.

parameters.name: white
parameters.description: The white value. 0 to 255.

parameters.name: fadeTime
parameters.description: The time to fade from the previous state in milliseconds.

functions.name: Phidget.SetLEDs
functions.signature: MBS( "Phidget.SetLEDs"; PhidgetRef; StartAddress; EndAddress; pattern; fadeTime )
functions.description: Sets the LEDs for a LED array. Only for LED arrays.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: StartAddress
parameters.description: The address of the first LED in the segment.

parameters.name: EndAddress
parameters.description: The address of the last LED in the segment.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: The list with colors. 4 values per color with red, green, blue and white.

parameters.name: fadeTime
parameters.description: The time to fade from the previous state in milliseconds.

functions.name: Phidget.SetProperty
functions.description: Sets a property for the phidget object. Please check phidgets documentation about what things which device has and what values are expected.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: PropertyName
parameters.description: The name of the property to set.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to pass. Depending on property, this can be a number or text. For enum properties, please pass the numeric value.

$r

( "Phidget.SetProperty";
$$phidget
; "Contrast"; ,5) ]

examples.label: Turn on a digital output by setting state to 1
examples.text_code:
# parameter is which phidget to use
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
If [ Length (
$$phidgets
[
$index
] ) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Phidget.SetProperty";
$$phidgets
[
$index
]; "State"; 1) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> parameter is which phidget to use</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( $$phidgets[$index] ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Phidget.SetProperty"; $$phidgets[$index]; "State"; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Phidget.SetScriptTrigger
functions.signature: MBS( "Phidget.SetScriptTrigger"; PhidgetRef; Event; FileName; ScriptName { ; Tag } )
functions.description: Installs a script trigger with the phidget. Please check phidgets documentation about what events are available for which device. The JSON passed to the script will contains an entry for ID with the passed phidget reference number. Available events:

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: Event
parameters.description: The name of the event.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of the FileMaker database containing the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name of the script to trigger.

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: Optional tag value. Goes into the JSON passed to the script, so you can pass whatever you like to the script.

examples.label: Set attach and detach script triggers:

$$phidget
; "Detach"; Get(FileName); "Detached" ) ]

functions.name: Phidget.WriteRFID
functions.signature: MBS( "Phidget.WriteRFID"; PhidgetRef; tagString { ; lockTag; protocol; chipset } )
functions.description: Writes data to the tag being currently read by the reader. You cannot write to a read-only or locked tag.

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: tagString
parameters.description: The data to write to the tag.

parameters.name: lockTag
parameters.description: If true, permanently locks the tag so that it cannot be re-written after this write. Default is false.

parameters.name: protocol

parameters.name: chipset

functions.name: Phidget.WriteText
functions.signature: MBS( "Phidget.WriteText"; PhidgetRef; Font; xPosition; yPosition; Text )
functions.description: Writes text to a LCD screen. This function writes text to the current frame buffer at the specified location. Changes made to the frame buffer must be flushed to the LCD screen using Phidget.Flush .

parameters.name: PhidgetRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the phidget.

parameters.name: Font
parameters.description: The font of the text. Pass 3 for 6x10 font, 4 for 5x8 font and 5 for 6x12 font.

parameters.name: xPosition

parameters.name: yPosition
parameters.description: The Y position of the start of the text string.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to be written.

examples.label: Output some text to LCD:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Phidget.Clear
";
$$phidget
) ]
# write some text
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Phidget.WriteText";
$$phidget
; 4; 0; 0; "Hello" ) ]
# and send all now
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Phidget.Flush
";
$$phidget
) ]

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>

      <Calculation>MBS( "Phidget.WriteText"; $$phidget; 4; 0; 0; "Hello" ) </Calculation>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Time.Format
functions.description: Formats a date with C time formatting functions. locale is the name of the locale to use. You can pass empty string to use default/current locale. Format is a format string like for strftime command in C. The format specification is a string and may contain special character sequences called conversion specifications, each of which is introduced by a '%' character and terminated by some other character known as a conversion specifier character. All other character sequences are ordinary character sequences. The characters of ordinary character sequences (including the null byte) are copied verbatim from format to s. However, the characters of conversion specifications are replaced as follows:

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The FileMaker time stamp.

parameters.name: FormatString
parameters.description: The format string.

parameters.name: Locale
parameters.description: The locale to use. Be aware that identifiers are not the same on Mac, Windows and Linux.

examples.label: Calculate time with time zone with double colon:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
t = MBS( "Time.Format"; Get ( CurrentTimeStamp ); "%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S %z");
l = Length ( t );
w = Position ( t ; ":" ; 1 ; 3 );
r = if(w > 0; t; Left ( t ; l-2 ) & ":" & Middle ( t ; l-2 ; 2 ))
]; t & ¶ & r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Time.Parse
functions.description: Parses a date. The Time.Parse function parses the string in the parameter, according to the string pointed to by format parameter, and returns the result as timestamp.  The resulting values will be relative to the local time zone.  Thus, it can be considered the reverse operation of Time.Format . The Time.Parse function is the converse function to Time.Format and converts the character string pointed to by s to values which are stored in the date, using the format specified by format. Here format is a character string that consists of field descriptors and text characters, reminiscent of scanf (in C++). Each field descriptor consists of a % character followed by another character that specifies the replacement for the field descriptor. All other characters in the format string must have a matching character in the input string, except for whitespace, which matches zero or more whitespace characters in the input string. There should be whitespace or other alphanumeric characters between any two field descriptors. The Time.Parse function processes the input string from left to right. Each of the three possible input elements (whitespace, literal, or format) are handled one after the other. If the input cannot be matched to the format string the function stops. The remainder of the format and input strings are not processed. The supported input field descriptors are listed below. In case a text string (such as a weekday or month name) is to be matched, the comparison is case insensitive. In case a number is to be matched, leading zeros are permitted but not required.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to parse for date and/or time

parameters.name: FormatString
parameters.description: The format string.

parameters.description: The locale to use. Be aware that identifiers are not the same on Mac, Windows and Linux.

functions.name: Time.Sleep
functions.description: Delays execution for the given number of milliseconds. You can use this to delay something on purpose. The while FileMaker Pro app is locked for the given time. Pass 1000 for a one second delay. This pauses execution of evaluation or a script. To avoid you block yourself endlessly, we limit the range to 1 to 60000 microseconds = one minute maximum.

parameters.name: Duration
parameters.description: Number of milliseconds to wait.

examples.label: Pause 5 milliseconds:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  t1 = Get(CurrentTimeUTCMilliseconds);
  r = MBS( "Time.Sleep"; 5 );
  t2 = Get(CurrentTimeUTCMilliseconds)
]; t1 & ¶ & t2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Time.UnixTimeStampToFileMaker
functions.description: Converts time from unix to FileMaker. Pass unix time, which is number of seconds since 1970.

parameters.name: UnixTime
parameters.description: The time value from unix.

parameters.name: GMT
parameters.description: Pass 1 for GMT or 0 for local time zone. Default is 0 for local time.

examples.label: Convert time:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Time.UnixTimeStampToFileMaker"; 1527786192 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Time.Wait
functions.description: Waits for the given number of milliseconds. You can use this to delay something on purpose. We loop and process events, so dialogs redraw and wait till time is over. Pass 1000 for a one second delay. This pauses execution of evaluation or a script. To avoid you block yourself endlessly, we limit the range to 1 to 60000 microseconds = one minute maximum. Time.Wait allows windows to draw, while Time.Sleep blocks all events for the delay.

parameters.name: Duration
parameters.description: The duration in milliseocnds.

examples.label: Waits 5 milliseconds:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  t1 = Get(CurrentTimeUTCMilliseconds);
  r = MBS( "Time.Wait"; 5 );
  t2 = Get(CurrentTimeUTCMilliseconds)
]; t1 & ¶ & t2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
